walkthrough.title: Unfold the Atlas Map
walkthrough.text: Let's set up for our adventure! First, unfold the large atlas map and spread it on the table or a nearby surface. This is the Wandering Sea, and it is divided into numbered regions. You will physically move your ship token across this map as you explore.

walkthrough.title: Place the Ship Board
walkthrough.text: Now set the ship board where everyone can reach it. This tracks everything about the Manticore, your steamship. It has spaces for crew positions, supply tokens, damage tokens, and command tokens. Think of it as your mission control.

walkthrough.title: Assign Crew Members
walkthrough.text: Find all nine crew cards. Each crew member has unique abilities, health tracks, and equipment slots. Captain Sofi Odessa is always in the crew. Place all nine crew members on the ship board in their starting positions with full health.

walkthrough.title: Divide Crew Among Players
walkthrough.text: Let's split up the crew. Each player controls a subset of the nine crew members, divided as evenly as possible. If playing solo, you control all nine. Discuss who wants which crew members, but keep in mind you will be responsible for their abilities and equipment.

walkthrough.title: Place Starting Supplies
walkthrough.text: Put the starting supply tokens on the ship board. Supplies represent food and resources for the crew. You will consume supplies every turn during the upkeep phase. Running out means your crew takes damage, so keep an eye on this throughout the campaign.

walkthrough.title: Set Command Tokens
walkthrough.text: Place the available command tokens on the ship board. Command tokens are your action boosters. You spend them during your turn to activate crew abilities, improve skill checks, or take bonus actions. They refresh at the start of each turn, so use them freely.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Card Decks
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the event deck, the challenge deck, and the equipment deck separately. Place them face down near the play area. The event deck drives random encounters, the challenge deck determines skill check difficulties, and equipment cards improve your crew.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Storybook
walkthrough.text: Keep the storybook nearby but closed. This spiral-bound book contains hundreds of numbered entries describing locations, encounters, and story events. You will reference specific entries as you explore locations on the atlas map. Do not read ahead.

walkthrough.title: Place the Ship on the Map
walkthrough.text: Put the Manticore ship token on the starting location indicated in the setup instructions. This is where your adventure begins. The atlas is an open world, so soon you will be choosing your own destinations.

walkthrough.title: Read the Introduction
walkthrough.text: Open the storybook to the introductory entry. Read it aloud to the group. This sets the narrative scene: Captain Sofi Odessa and her crew are lost in a strange world and need to find fourteen totems to return home. The introduction also gives you your first clues about where to go.

walkthrough.title: Understand the Save System
walkthrough.text: Sleeping Gods is a campaign game spanning ten to twenty hours across multiple sessions. At the end of any session, you can save by recording your ship position, crew health, equipment, quest progress, and totems found. Use the provided bags to store tokens between sessions.

walkthrough.title: Begin Your First Turn
walkthrough.text: You are ready to begin! Your first turn starts with an event card, then you take two actions as a group. You might travel to a nearby region, explore your current location, or visit a market. Let's learn how turns work in the rules section.

summary.title: Begin Your First Turn
bullets: Event card → 2 group actions per turn
Actions: travel, explore, market, rest, equip

walkthrough.title: Campaign Overview
walkthrough.text: Sleeping Gods is a cooperative campaign adventure. You control Captain Sofi Odessa and her crew aboard the steamship Manticore, lost in a strange world. Your goal is to explore the atlas map, follow story threads in the storybook, and find fourteen totems needed to return home. The more totems you find, the better your ending.

walkthrough.title: Turn Structure: Event Phase
walkthrough.text: Draw an event card from the top of the event deck. Events can be beneficial, harmful, or trigger story moments. Some events damage the ship or injure crew members. Read the event aloud and resolve it before moving to your actions.

walkthrough.title: Turn Structure: Action Phase
walkthrough.text: After the event, your group takes two actions. All players discuss and decide together. You can take the same action twice or two different actions. The five main actions are: travel, explore, market, rest, and equip. Let's walk through each one.

walkthrough.title: Action: Travel
walkthrough.text: Move the ship to an adjacent region on the atlas map. Each region is numbered and has connections to neighboring regions. You can only move to regions directly connected to your current location. Travel is how you reach new areas to explore.

walkthrough.title: Action: Explore
walkthrough.text: At your current location, look up the corresponding numbered entry in the storybook. Read it aloud and follow the instructions. Entries often present choices like helping or ignoring someone, fighting or negotiating, or exploring deeper. Your choices have real consequences.

walkthrough.title: Action: Market
walkthrough.text: If a market is available at your current location, you can buy and sell equipment and supplies. Markets are your main source of resupply. Sell unneeded equipment to fund purchases. Keep your crew equipped and your ship stocked.

walkthrough.title: Action: Rest
walkthrough.text: Rest heals your crew members by removing damage tokens. Keeping your crew healthy is essential because injured crew members are less effective in skill checks and combat. If too many crew are incapacitated simultaneously, the campaign can end.

walkthrough.title: Action: Equip
walkthrough.text: Assign or rearrange equipment among crew members. Each crew member has limited equipment slots. Weapons improve attack capabilities. Armor reduces incoming damage. Tools provide bonuses to skill checks. Match equipment to each crew member's strengths.

walkthrough.title: Turn Structure: Upkeep Phase
walkthrough.text: After your two actions, the upkeep phase occurs. The crew consumes supplies, which represents feeding them. If you cannot provide enough supplies, your crew takes damage. Always keep at least a few turns worth of supplies in reserve.

walkthrough.title: Command Tokens
walkthrough.text: These are your most flexible resource. Spend them during actions to activate crew abilities, boost skill checks, or take extra actions. They refresh at the start of each turn, so there is no reason to hoard them. Use them every turn for maximum efficiency.

walkthrough.title: Skill Checks
walkthrough.text: When the storybook calls for a skill check, draw challenge cards showing a target number. Use your crew members' stats plus equipment bonuses to meet or exceed the target. Success and failure lead to different story outcomes. Some checks have tiered results.

walkthrough.title: Combat Overview
walkthrough.text: Some storybook entries lead to combat. Enemies are placed on a combat grid with hit zones like head, body, and legs. Each crew member attacks using their weapon stats, targeting specific zones. Enemies attack back based on patterns on their cards. Damaged crew flip face down until healed.

walkthrough.title: Keywords and Story Branching
walkthrough.text: As you make choices in the storybook, you earn keywords. Write these down because they unlock specific entries later. Some locations have hidden paths only accessible with certain keywords. This creates a branching narrative based on your previous decisions.

walkthrough.title: Ship Damage and Crew Health
walkthrough.text: Both the Manticore and your crew take damage from events and combat. Repair the ship by spending resources and actions. Heal crew by resting. Incapacitated crew cannot act. If too many crew are down at once, or the ship takes too much damage, the campaign can end early.

walkthrough.title: Totem Collection
walkthrough.text: The fourteen totems are hidden throughout the world, found through exploration, quests, and story choices. Track how many you have found. More totems lead to a better ending. Some totems are easy to find, while others require specific keywords or completing quest chains.

walkthrough.title: Campaign End and Endings
walkthrough.text: The campaign ends when you find all fourteen totems, the event deck runs out, too many crew are incapacitated, or you choose to attempt the final challenge. Your totem count determines the ending quality. Zero to four totems is a bad ending. Five to eight is mediocre. Nine to twelve is good. Thirteen to fourteen is the best ending.

Find 14 totems → more totems = better ending

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Wandering Sea
walkthrough.text: We will walk through the opening turns of a two-player campaign. Player one controls five crew members, and Player two controls four. Let's get the Manticore sailing and see how the game flows.

walkthrough.title: Your First Event
walkthrough.text: Let's begin. Draw an event card. It says a storm is approaching. The ship takes one damage. Place a damage token on the ship board. Events like this are common and add unpredictability. Resolve it and move on to your actions.

walkthrough.title: First Action: Explore
walkthrough.text: Look up the numbered entry in the storybook and read it aloud. The entry describes a small coastal village with a market and a mysterious temple on the hill. It gives you a choice: visit the market or investigate the temple.

walkthrough.title: Making Your First Choice
walkthrough.text: Let's investigate the temple. The storybook entry leads to a skill check. Draw a challenge card, and the target is eight. Player one sends their strongest crew member with a diplomacy stat of five, plus an equipment bonus of two, totaling seven. Close, but not enough.

walkthrough.title: Using Command Tokens
walkthrough.text: Player two spends a command token to boost the check by one, pushing the total to eight. Success! The storybook describes finding an ancient inscription that grants you a keyword. Write it down. This keyword will unlock new entries later. Command tokens are powerful, so use them when it matters.

walkthrough.title: Second Action: Travel
walkthrough.text: Look at the atlas and pick a direction. The introductory quest hints at something interesting to the east. Move the Manticore to the eastern region. You will explore it next turn.

walkthrough.title: Upkeep: Feed the Crew
walkthrough.text: Actions done. Now the upkeep phase. Remove supply tokens from the ship board to feed the crew. You have plenty right now, but keep an eye on the supply level. Running out of food is one of the most common ways to lose crew health over a campaign.

walkthrough.title: Turn Two: A Combat Encounter
walkthrough.text: New turn, new event. This one is peaceful, so nothing happens. For your first action, explore the new region. The storybook entry leads to a hostile encounter. Enemies appear! Place enemy standees on the combat grid.

walkthrough.title: Player One's Mistake: Ignoring Hit Zones
walkthrough.text: Player one attacks an enemy but targets its armored body zone instead of its vulnerable legs. The body has high defense, so the attack does minimal damage. Meanwhile, the legs have low defense and would have taken full damage. Targeting matters in Sleeping Gods.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Target Vulnerable Zones
walkthrough.text: Always check enemy hit zones before attacking. Each zone has different defense values. Targeting vulnerable zones lets you deal full damage with fewer resources. Wasting attacks on armored zones means the fight drags on and your crew takes more damage in return.

walkthrough.title: Player Two's Mistake: Hoarding Command Tokens
walkthrough.text: During the combat, Player two has three command tokens but refuses to spend any, saving them for later. The crew struggles through the fight, taking unnecessary damage. At the end of the turn, command tokens refresh anyway. Those three tokens went completely to waste.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Spend Command Tokens Every Turn
walkthrough.text: Command tokens refresh at the start of each turn. There is absolutely no benefit to saving them. Use every single one every turn. Boost skill checks, activate crew abilities, and take bonus actions. Hoarding command tokens is leaving free resources on the table.

walkthrough.title: Player One's Mistake: Pushing Through Injuries
walkthrough.text: After combat, several crew members are injured. Player one decides to keep exploring instead of resting, hoping to find a totem. The next exploration triggers another combat. With injured crew, the fight goes badly. Two more crew members get incapacitated.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Rest Before You Are Desperate
walkthrough.text: Do not push through injuries hoping for the best. An injured crew is an ineffective crew. Rest when you have two or more crew members damaged. It is better to spend one action resting than to lose multiple crew members in a fight you were not prepared for.

walkthrough.title: Recovery and Regrouping
walkthrough.text: The group decides to rest and visit a market. They heal crew, sell extra equipment for supplies, and restock. Smart play. Sometimes the best adventure is the one where you take a breath and prepare before pushing forward.

walkthrough.title: Finding Your First Totem
walkthrough.text: After regrouping, the team explores a mysterious island. A series of storybook entries, aided by the keyword earned earlier, leads to a hidden shrine. Inside is one of the fourteen totems. One down, thirteen to go. The keyword from the temple was the key.

walkthrough.title: Saving Your Progress
walkthrough.text: After a few more turns, the session is wrapping up. Let's save. Record the ship position, all crew health and equipment, quest progress, keywords earned, totems found, and supply levels. Bag your tokens and close the storybook. Next session picks up right where you left off.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations, Adventurers
walkthrough.text: Congratulations! You have played your first session of Sleeping Gods. You explored, made choices, fought in combat, found a totem, and learned from three key mistakes. Target vulnerable zones, spend command tokens every turn, and rest before you are desperate.

walkthrough.title: Before Your Next Session
walkthrough.text:
Before you continue your campaign, here are three things to help.

First, check out General Tips for exploration strategy and resource management.

Second, reference the Appendix for quick definitions of game terms and mechanics.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

bullets: Check out General Tips → exploration and resource management
Reference the Appendix → game terms and mechanics
Ask your personal GM Guide → tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Explore Broadly Before Focusing
walkthrough.text: In your first few sessions, resist the urge to follow one quest line straight to the end. Visit many different locations to gather clues, equipment, and keywords. The more keywords you collect early, the more options open up later. Broad exploration in the early game pays dividends in the mid and late game.

walkthrough.title: Keep Your Crew Healthy
walkthrough.text: An injured crew is an ineffective crew. When two or more crew members are damaged, seriously consider taking a rest action. Pushing through with injured crew leads to failed skill checks and costly combats. It is always better to heal proactively than to scramble after a disaster.

walkthrough.title: Manage Supplies Carefully
walkthrough.text: Running out of food is one of the most devastating things that can happen. Always maintain at least two or three turns worth of supplies. When you are near a market with excess equipment, sell unneeded items for food. Plan your travel routes so you pass through resupply points.

walkthrough.title: Read Storybook Entries Carefully
walkthrough.text: The storybook hides clues about totem locations, quest resolutions, and hidden paths. Pay attention to names, directions, and descriptions. If an entry mentions a landmark to the north, make a note. Many players miss important information by skimming instead of reading carefully.

walkthrough.title: Take Notes
walkthrough.text: Keep a notebook during your campaign. Write down interesting locations, non-player character names, unresolved quest threads, and any clues about totem locations. Over a ten to twenty hour campaign, you will forget details. Good notes save you from backtracking and wasted turns.

walkthrough.title: Don't Fight Everything
walkthrough.text: Some combats are optional. If the storybook gives you a choice between fighting and retreating, consider the state of your crew. A wounded, under-equipped crew gains nothing from a pyrrhic victory. Retreat when the cost of fighting outweighs the reward.

walkthrough.title: Equip Your Crew Wisely
walkthrough.text: Give weapons to your best fighters and tools to your skill-check specialists. Do not spread equipment randomly. A crew member with high strength gets more value from a weapon than one with high diplomacy. Match equipment to stats for maximum effectiveness.

walkthrough.title: Use Command Tokens Aggressively
walkthrough.text: Command tokens refresh every turn. There is no strategic value in saving them. Use all of them every single turn. Boost your most important skill check. Activate a crew ability that turns the tide of combat. Take a bonus action to squeeze one more exploration in. Unused command tokens are wasted potential.

summary.title: Explore Broadly Before Focusing
bullets: Visit many locations early for clues, equipment, keywords
More keywords = more options later
Broad early exploration pays off in mid/late game

summary.title: Keep Your Crew Healthy
bullets: Rest when 2+ crew are damaged
Injured crew fail checks and lose combats
Proactive healing > scrambling after disaster

summary.title: Manage Supplies Carefully
bullets: Maintain 2-3 turns of supplies minimum
Sell unneeded equipment at markets
Plan routes through resupply points

summary.title: Read Storybook Entries Carefully
bullets: Clues hidden in descriptions and names
Note mentions of landmarks and directions
Skimming = missed totem clues

summary.title: Take Notes
bullets: Write down NPCs, locations, quest threads, clues
10-20 hour campaign = you will forget details
Good notes prevent backtracking

summary.title: Don't Fight Everything
bullets: Some combats are optional — retreat is valid
Wounded crew gains nothing from pyrrhic victory
Weigh cost of fighting vs reward

summary.title: Equip Your Crew Wisely
bullets: Weapons → best fighters; tools → skill-check specialists
Match equipment to crew stats
Don't spread equipment randomly

summary.title: Use Command Tokens Aggressively
bullets: Refresh every turn — no value in saving
Use ALL tokens every turn
Unused tokens = wasted potential

walkthrough.title: Systematic Exploration
walkthrough.text: Rather than wandering randomly, work through regions methodically. Clear one area of the map before moving to the next. This ensures you do not miss hidden entries and maximizes your keyword collection. Systematic coverage is more efficient than chasing distant quest markers.

walkthrough.title: Keyword Revisiting
walkthrough.text: When you earn new keywords, mentally review locations you have already visited. Some entries that gave you nothing the first time have hidden paths unlocked by keywords earned later. Revisiting with the right keyword can reveal totems, powerful equipment, or critical quest progress.

walkthrough.title: Quest Thread Prioritization
walkthrough.text: Quests often lead to totems. Prioritize following quest chains to completion over random exploration. When you have multiple active quests, focus on the one that seems closest to resolution. Unfinished quests at campaign end yield nothing.

walkthrough.title: Combat Zone Targeting
walkthrough.text: In combat, always target the most vulnerable zone on each enemy first. Hit vulnerable zones to kill enemies faster and reduce incoming damage. Use command tokens to boost attacks on critical targets rather than spreading damage across multiple enemies. Focus fire wins fights.

walkthrough.title: Crew Specialization
walkthrough.text: As your crew levels up, specialize them into distinct roles. Have one or two combat specialists, one or two diplomacy experts, and one perception specialist. This ensures you can handle any skill check the storybook throws at you without needing the entire crew present.

walkthrough.title: Supply Route Planning
walkthrough.text: Map out the locations of markets and supply sources across the atlas. Plan your exploration routes to loop back through resupply points every few turns. Running out of supplies in a remote region with no market nearby can spiral into a campaign-ending crisis.

walkthrough.title: Event Deck Awareness
walkthrough.text: The event deck is your campaign timer. As it shrinks, the campaign approaches its end. Track the remaining events roughly. When the deck gets low, shift from broad exploration to focused totem hunting. Every turn spent on side content near the end is a turn not spent finding totems.

walkthrough.title: Session Pacing
walkthrough.text: Each session, set a goal for how many totems you want to find. Aim for two or three per session depending on session length. This keeps the campaign on pace for a good ending. If you are behind on totems mid-campaign, prioritize quest chains over exploration.

summary.title: Systematic Exploration
bullets: Clear one map area before moving to the next
Maximizes keyword collection
More efficient than chasing distant markers

summary.title: Keyword Revisiting
bullets: New keywords may unlock entries at previous locations
Revisiting can reveal totems or powerful equipment
Review visited locations when earning new keywords

summary.title: Quest Thread Prioritization
bullets: Quests lead to totems — follow chains to completion
Focus on quests closest to resolution
Unfinished quests at campaign end yield nothing

summary.title: Combat Zone Targeting
bullets: Target most vulnerable zones first
Focus fire to kill enemies faster
Use command tokens on critical attacks

summary.title: Crew Specialization
bullets: Specialize crew into combat, diplomacy, perception roles
Ensures coverage for any skill check type
Level up specialists for maximum stat growth

summary.title: Supply Route Planning
bullets: Map market locations across the atlas
Plan routes to loop through resupply points
Remote exploration without supply access = crisis

summary.title: Event Deck Awareness
bullets: Event deck = campaign timer
Track remaining events roughly
Low deck → shift to focused totem hunting

summary.title: Session Pacing
bullets: Aim for 2-3 totems per session
Behind on totems → prioritize quests over exploration
Keeps campaign on pace for good ending

entries.definition: A large fold-out map of the Wandering Sea divided into numbered regions. Players physically move the ship token across this map during travel actions.
A spiral-bound book with hundreds of numbered entries. Each location on the atlas corresponds to a storybook entry describing events, choices, and consequences.
Tracks the Manticore's condition including crew positions, supplies, damage, and command tokens. Central hub for all resource management.
The steamship your crew sails aboard. It has its own damage track. If the ship takes too much damage, the campaign can end early.
Represents one of nine crew members. Each has unique stats (strength, perception, diplomacy, stealth), health track, equipment slots, and special abilities.
The protagonist and captain of the Manticore. Always part of the crew. Has her own unique abilities and story role.
One of fourteen collectible items needed to return home. Found through exploration, quests, and story choices. More totems collected leads to a better ending.
A narrative flag earned through story choices. Keywords unlock specific storybook entries at other locations. Write them down as you earn them.
A flexible resource that refreshes each turn. Spend to activate crew abilities, boost skill checks, or take bonus actions. Always use all tokens every turn.
Represents food and resources for the crew. Consumed during upkeep each turn. Running out causes crew damage.
Drawn at the start of each turn. Events can be beneficial, harmful, or trigger story moments. The event deck also serves as the campaign timer.
Drawn during skill checks to determine the target number the crew must meet or exceed. Difficulty varies based on the card drawn.
Weapons, armor, and tools that improve crew capabilities. Found through exploration, markets, and quest rewards. Each crew member has limited slots.
Tracks ongoing objectives and story progress. Quests often lead to totems and powerful rewards when completed.
An action to move the ship to an adjacent region on the atlas map. Required to reach new exploration locations.
An action to read the storybook entry for your current location. Triggers narrative choices, skill checks, and potential combat encounters.
An action available at certain locations to buy and sell equipment and supplies. Main source of resupply during the campaign.
An action to heal crew members by removing damage tokens. Essential for maintaining crew effectiveness.
An action to assign or rearrange equipment among crew members. Match weapons, armor, and tools to each crew member's strengths.
A test where crew stats plus equipment bonuses must meet or exceed a target number from a challenge card. Success and failure lead to different story outcomes.
The layout where enemies are placed during combat encounters. Enemies have multiple hit zones with varying defense values.
Specific areas on enemies (head, body, legs) that can be targeted in combat. Each zone has different defense values. Target vulnerable zones for maximum damage.
A crew member whose health has reached zero. Incapacitated crew cannot act and must be healed through resting. Too many incapacitated at once can end the campaign.
Record ship position, crew state, quests, keywords, totems, and supplies between sessions. Use provided bags for token storage. Resume next session from saved state.
The phase at the end of each turn where the crew consumes supplies. If supplies run out, crew members take damage.
Determined by the number of totems found. Bad (0-4), mediocre (5-8), good (9-12), best (13-14). Read the corresponding storybook entry at campaign end.
